canonical_definition: A real-world multimodal dataset combining video and text to assess machine learning models’ ability to infer mental states, used here to validate the generalizability of probing methods.
papers: From Black Boxes to Transparent Minds: Evaluating and Enhancing the Theory of Mind in Multimodal Large Language Models
Overcoming Multi-step Complexity in Multimodal Theory-of-Mind Reasoning: A Scalable Bayesian Planner

definitions.text: A real-world multimodal dataset combining video and text to assess machine learning models’ ability to infer mental states, used here to validate the generalizability of probing methods.
definitions.paper: From Black Boxes to Transparent Minds: Evaluating and Enhancing the Theory of Mind in Multimodal Large Language Models

relationships.paper: Overcoming Multi-step Complexity in Multimodal Theory-of-Mind Reasoning: A Scalable Bayesian Planner
relationships.evidence: The MMToM-QA dataset (Jin et al., 2024) is the pioneering resource aimed at assessing machine learning models’ ability to infer mental states from multimodal data, combining video and text in real-world tasks.

icml25: Overcoming Multi-step Complexity in Multimodal Theory-of-Mind Reasoning: A Scalable Bayesian Planner
From Black Boxes to Transparent Minds: Evaluating and Enhancing the Theory of Mind in Multimodal Large Language Models
